mission.title: Resolve RBAC permission errors when the console reports RBAC_DENIED
mission.description: Repair mission for Kubernetes authorization failures. Use it when the console preflight reports RBAC_DENIED, when kubectl returns Forbidden errors, or when a user or service account needs a targeted RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding to perform the intended action.

steps.title: Identify the active subject and failing namespace
steps.description: Determine which Kubernetes identity is being denied and which namespace or cluster-scoped resource triggered the RBAC failure.
commands:
kubectl config current-context
kubectl auth whoami
echo "TARGET_NAMESPACE=${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"

steps.title: Check what the subject can do right now
steps.description: Use kubectl auth can-i to measure the current effective permissions for the verb and resource that failed, plus a broader namespace permission inventory.
commands:
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"
kubectl auth can-i --list -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"
kubectl auth can-i get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"
kubectl auth can-i create deployments.apps -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"

steps.title: Find the relevant roles and existing bindings
steps.description: Inspect existing RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings so you can reuse an appropriate built-in role such as view, edit, or admin instead of guessing or over-privileging.
commands:
kubectl get rolebindings -A -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,SUBJECTS:.subjects[*].name
kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,SUBJECTS:.subjects[*].name
kubectl get clusterroles view edit admin -o name

steps.title: Create a targeted RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding
steps.description: Bind the active identity to the smallest role that grants the missing permission. Use a namespaced RoleBinding for namespace resources, or a ClusterRoleBinding only if the denied resource is cluster scoped.
commands:
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"
AUDIT_SUBJECT="${AUDIT_SUBJECT:-$(kubectl auth whoami -o jsonpath='{.status.userInfo.username}')}"
kubectl create rolebinding console-view --clusterrole=view --user="$AUDIT_SUBJECT" -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
AUDIT_SUBJECT="${AUDIT_SUBJECT:-$(kubectl auth whoami -o jsonpath='{.status.userInfo.username}')}"
kubectl create clusterrolebinding console-read-nodes --clusterrole=view --user="$AUDIT_SUBJECT" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

steps.title: Verify the permission gap is closed
steps.description: Repeat the specific authorization check and a low-risk read operation to confirm RBAC_DENIED is resolved without granting unnecessary privileges.
commands:
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"
kubectl auth can-i get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-default}"
kubectl get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --request-timeout=15s
kubectl auth can-i get nodes

troubleshooting.title: The workload runs as a service account
troubleshooting.description: Set AUDIT_SUBJECT to system:serviceaccount:<namespace>:<name> or create the RoleBinding with --serviceaccount=<namespace>:<name>; a human user binding will not help a pod identity.

troubleshooting.title: The denied resource is cluster scoped
troubleshooting.description: Resources such as nodes, namespaces, and CRDs require a ClusterRole plus ClusterRoleBinding. A namespaced RoleBinding cannot grant access to cluster-scoped resources.

troubleshooting.title: You cannot list bindings to diagnose the problem
troubleshooting.description: Save the exact Forbidden message and ask a cluster administrator to inspect RBAC resources. If even kubectl auth can-i is blocked, the remediation must be performed by a more privileged identity.

resolution.summary: The denied subject, missing permission, and appropriate binding were identified, and a least-privilege RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding was applied to resolve RBAC_DENIED.
codeSnippets: kubectl create rolebinding console-view --clusterrole=view --user="$AUDIT_SUBJECT" -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

prerequisites.resources: A kubeconfig that can reach the cluster API server; write access is required only for the binding step.
prerequisites.description: Set TARGET_NAMESPACE and AUDIT_SUBJECT when the denied action came from a different namespace or from a service account.
